name: La Pedrera reservoir and Lagunas de La Mata and Torrevieja: loop from Torrevieja
name_original: Embalse de La Pedrera y lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja — Circular desde Torrevieja
description:
The adventure is circular from a car park in Torrevieja, very close to the natural park of the Lagunas de La Mata and Torrevieja. Between the views of the pink waters of the Torrevieja lagoon, salt flats and palm groves, you advance towards the only climb of the day. Almost all the gradient of this route is accumulated in a pleasant ascent through the Mediterranean forest and almond plantations between San Miguel de Salinas and the port of Rebate.

From this height, where the views are spectacular, you descend through a sea of plots of almond and olive trees. The fertility of the landscape contradicts its desert appearance. Further on you find yourself before the turquoise views of the waters of the La Pedrera reservoir, surrounded by a sea of citrus trees. Between endless plantations and irrigation channels you reach the lagoons of Torrevieja and La Mata, an oasis for aquatic birds, especially flamingos. The route takes you to several viewpoints where you can take photos.

The route skirts, but does not enter, the different towns. This is why it is essential that you bring with you all the hydration and food you may need during the day. If you prefer, you can make a detour in San Miguel de Salinas, to make a brief stop.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Torrevieja offers diverse terrain, characterized by its unique salt lakes, coastal areas, and natural parks. The region features the striking pink Laguna Rosa and the greener La Mata Lagoon, both surrounded by trails. Gravel routes also extend to the La Pedrera Reservoir and through Mediterranean scrubland, providing varied landscapes for riders. The terrain ranges from flat and gently undulating paths around the lagoons to more challenging sections with some elevation changes. Best gravel bike trails around Torrevieja The most popular gravel bike trail is La Pedrera reservoir and Lagunas de La Mata and Torrevieja: loop from Torrevieja, a 38.4 miles (61.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route combines the reservoir with the unique salt lagoons. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is La Mata Lagoon – Segura River Bridge loop from Villa Martín, a difficult 47.0 miles (75.6 km) path. This route explores the areas around La Mata Lagoon and the Segura River. Local gravel bikers also love the Cabezo de la Plata Summit – La Pedrera Reservoir loop from Villa Martín, a 55.2 miles (88.8 km) trail leading through the La Pedrera Reservoir area and towards a summit, often completed in about 6 hours 4 minutes. Gravel biking around Torrevieja is defined by its salt lagoons, Mediterranean scrub, and coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 180 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Torrevieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Torrevieja?
A: There are over 70 gravel bike trails around Torrevieja, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy loops to more challenging, longer rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Torrevieja?
A: The terrain around Torrevieja is quite diverse, characterized by its unique salt lakes, coastal paths, and Mediterranean scrubland. You'll encounter flat and gently undulating paths, especially around the lagoons, but also more challenging sections with some elevation changes, particularly when venturing towards areas like the La Pedrera Reservoir. Some segments might be unpaved or have deeper sand.

Q: Are there any easy gravel bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Torrevieja?
A: Yes, Torrevieja offers several easy gravel routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31860507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de La Mata – Laguna de La Mata loop from Corinto&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.9-mile (24.0 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 47 minutes to complete, providing a gentle introduction to the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What are the most scenic spots or natural attractions to see while gravel biking in Torrevieja?
A: Torrevieja is rich in natural beauty. While gravel biking, you can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/771712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata and Torrevieja Lagoons Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, home to the iconic pink &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6788952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Salada de Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt; and the greener &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/864166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. These areas offer unique landscapes and excellent birdwatching opportunities. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narrow trail around the Laguna de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, providing picturesque views.

Q: Can I see wildlife, like flamingos, while gravel biking in Torrevieja?
A: Absolutely! The Natural Park of the Lagoons of La Mata and Torrevieja is a vital ecosystem attracting over 100 bird species, including numerous pink flamingos, stilts, and avocets. Cycling around the salt lagoons, especially La Mata Lagoon, offers excellent opportunities for birdwatching and experiencing the region's unique flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any circular gravel routes available in Torrevieja?
A: Many of the gravel routes in Torrevieja are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922599238/la-pedrera-reservoir-and-lagunas-de-la-mata-and-torrevieja-loop-from-torrevieja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedrera reservoir and Lagunas de La Mata and Torrevieja: loop from Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt;, which combines the reservoir with the unique salt lagoons for a comprehensive ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Torrevieja?
A: Torrevieja boasts a healthy climate with around 320 days of sunshine annually, making it ideal for year-round outdoor pursuits. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for gravel biking, avoiding the peak heat of summer. Winter is also a great option, with mild temperatures perfect for exploring the trails.

Q: Where can I park my car when accessing gravel bike trails in Torrevieja?
A: Many trailheads and natural park entrances around Torrevieja offer designated parking areas. For routes starting from the city, you can often find street parking or public car parks. When planning your ride, it's advisable to check the specific starting point of your chosen route for the most convenient parking options.

Q: Are there any permits required to cycle in the natural parks around Torrevieja?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for cycling on the established trails within the Natural Park of the Lagoons of La Mata and Torrevieja. However, it's always good practice to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of wildlife. For detailed information, you can refer to the official park website: &lt;a href="https://www.spain.info/en/nature/lagunas-mata-torrevieja-natural-park/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Lagoons of La Mata and Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy the most about gravel biking in Torrevieja?
A: The gravel biking experience in Torrevieja is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique landscapes, especially the salt lagoons, the diverse terrain, and the opportunity to explore both natural parks and coastal areas. The variety of routes, from easy to challenging, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any advanced or challenging gravel routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced gravel bikers will find challenging routes around Torrevieja. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23743152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata Lagoon – Segura River Waterwheel loop from Jardín Adyacente&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 50.9-mile (82.0 km) path that explores the areas around La Mata Lagoon and the Segura River, requiring good fitness and offering a more demanding ride.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the gravel bike trails?
A: Torrevieja and its surrounding towns offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. While specific amenities directly on remote trail sections might be limited, you'll find plenty of choices in the urban centers and villages close to the natural parks and coastal areas, perfect for refueling after your ride or for an overnight stay.
tips: Along this section near San Miguel de Salinas you can pedal peacefully and enjoy the views of the Conesa Castle. An old fortress of Muslim origin that today acts as a restaurant and venue for events.
The Rebate pass is a small hill in the interior of Alicante that runs between orchards, pine forests, winding curves and spectacular views of the surrounding mountains.
